reference_audit.notes: Tests use only in-scope imported RFC 9000 IDs. No source-side requirement refs were present in the scoped code, which is consistent with the repository convention for this slice.

requirements.notes: Cryptographically unpredictable challenge bytes are generated with RandomNumberGenerator.Fill before the frame is formatted.
Datagrams that carry PATH_CHALLENGE are expanded to the 1200-byte minimum when the remaining anti-amplification budget permits.
The helper provides the unpredictable payload primitive that a caller uses to build a PATH_CHALLENGE for path validation.
PATH_RESPONSE echo behavior is exercised by the existing frame codec and verified by the new round-trip test.
PATH_RESPONSE-bearing datagrams are padded to the 1200-byte minimum when the anti-amplification budget allows it.
The padding helper refuses to expand a datagram once the remaining send budget would be exceeded.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no packet-composition surface yet to model arbitrary companion frames alongside PATH_CHALLENGE or PATH_RESPONSE.
requirements.notes: This is a packet-assembly concern, not a frame-codec concern, so it remains outside the available helper slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no send-path or packet-batching surface that can intentionally emit multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames when loss recovery requires it.
requirements.notes: The current code can build a single PATH_CHALLENGE frame, but not the packet-level strategy for duplicating it.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no packet composer yet to enforce or verify the one-PATH_CHALLENGE-per-packet guidance.
requirements.notes: The packet-level packing policy is not present in this slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no PTO/cadence scheduler or path-probing policy to compare PATH_CHALLENGE emission frequency against Initial packets.
requirements.notes: This remains a connection-state and recovery concern.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no second-validation trigger or MTU-confirmation state machine that can issue a follow-up PATH_CHALLENGE after the first probe succeeds.
requirements.notes: The helper can pad a datagram, but the orchestration for a second validation pass is missing.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no datagram-discard policy yet for path-validation frames that appear to arrive inside a too-small packet.
requirements.notes: Discard-vs-accept decisions for undersized packets are not implemented in the current path-validation slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no congestion-control integration on the send path, so PATH_RESPONSE timing cannot be constrained relative to congestion signals.
requirements.notes: This needs connection-level send scheduling rather than frame encoding helpers.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no per-path routing state to ensure PATH_RESPONSE is sent back on the same network path that carried the PATH_CHALLENGE.
requirements.notes: The current code has no path-selection or path-binding layer.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no responder/initiator policy surface to assert or relax the path-binding rule at the initiator side.
requirements.notes: The repo slice lacks the path-validation orchestration where this SHOULD NOT rule would be represented.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no challenge/response correlation state to limit PATH_RESPONSE fan-out to a single response per PATH_CHALLENGE.
requirements.notes: Response tracking is absent, so one-to-one fan-out cannot be enforced yet.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no challenge/response correlation state to limit PATH_RESPONSE fan-out to a single response per PATH_CHALLENGE.
requirements.notes: This is the same missing dependency as REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P2-0007.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no MTU-validation state machine that can trigger a second path validation with an expanded datagram.
requirements.notes: The helper can generate padding, but the MTU probe/retry orchestration is still missing.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no timer-based path-validation abandonment machinery in the current send/recovery surface.
requirements.notes: Timer control is outside the current helper-level implementation.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no NO_VIABLE_PATH connection-error surface to signal the absence of a valid network path.
requirements.notes: The repository slice does not yet expose the connection error that this MAY clause references.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no higher-level path-validation failure path that can decide when to emit NO_VIABLE_PATH.
requirements.notes: This remains blocked by the missing connection-error and timer orchestration surfaces.

notes: No reconciliation artifact existed for this chunk; the implementation summary was treated as the source of truth.
The helper-level implementation covers unpredictable PATH_CHALLENGE payload generation, response echoing, and datagram padding.
The remaining requirements depend on missing packet-assembly, path-routing, timer, and connection-error surfaces.
No stale or wrong requirement refs were found in the scoped source or tests.
